Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to consider typical sequence Post Job Ad Screen Candidates Conduct Interviews Make Hiring Decision Onboard Employee Add to Payroll Close Case Usually screening before interview interview after screening decision after interview onboarding after decision payroll after onboarding close case last

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model1 StrictPartialOrder nodes Post Screen Decide Interview Onboard Payroll Close Edges Post to Screen Screen to Decide Screen to Interview Decide to Onboard Onboard to Payroll Payroll to Close So order Post before Screen But then both Decide and Interview depend on Screen that is can happen in parallel Decision depends on Screening but not on Interview no edge from Interview to Decide or vice versa

Name: Validation - PE
Text: That violates typical logic because decision should come after interview Also there is no edge from Interview to Onboard or Payroll etc but that is okay if they are independent In this model Onboarding depends only on Decision So if Interview occurs later than Decision possible due to partial order then onboarding may start before interviews finish anomaly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model2 uses loop onboarding with Onboard and skip meaning repeated onboarding is possible xor payroll with Payroll and skip meaning either do payroll or skip it

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This introduces optional payroll step which deviates strongly because adding employee to payroll is essential for new hire process skipping it would mean the employee not paid properly anomaly severe

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Edges Post to Screen Post to Interview meaning screen and interview can happen concurrently right after posting job ad without sequencing them properly usually screen first then interview based on screened candidates only Having them both directly dependent only on post means they could be executed simultaneously or in any order relative each other anomaly

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The loop around onboard may allow repeating the same activity multiple times without condition unnatural unless modeling iterative onboarding steps but not typical normative process where each new hire goes through onboarding once

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model1 has anomaly of missing precedence between interview and decision leading potential early decisions before interviews complete However it still includes all mandatory activities exactly once and preserves most of the expected chain

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model2 has more severe anomalies optional skipping of payroll repeated onboarding and missing sequencing between screening and interview

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore Model1 aligns better with normative Hire to Retire process because its flaw is mainly one missing dependency while Model2 permits omission of an essential business step and adds less justified control flow complexity
